trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Brazil exports soybeans, iron ore, and chemicals to Malaysia. Malaysia exports palm oil, electronics, rubber, and liquefied natural gas to Brazil.

diplomatic.summary: Normal diplomatic relations. Both countries cooperate in G20 and multilateral forums. Shared interest in tropical forest conservation despite competition in palm oil vs. soy markets. Both pursue strategic autonomy.

military.summary: Very limited military ties. No alliance. Minimal defense cooperation.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Petronas has explored Brazilian energy markets. Some Malaysian conglomerates have modest Brazilian presence.

energy.summary: Malaysia exports LNG to Brazil. Some cooperation on bioenergy. Limited direct energy partnership.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific collaboration. Some joint research in tropical agriculture, forestry, and biodiversity.
